name: Almedina — circular from Villanueva de los Infantes
name_original: Almedina — circular desde Villanueva de los Infantes
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villanueva De Los Infantes traverse the historical Campo de Montiel region, characterized by its agricultural landscapes, charming towns, and proximity to natural features. The terrain generally consists of well-paved surfaces, with some routes featuring moderate elevation gains. Cyclists can explore areas rich in history and culture, including historical castles and picturesque natural settings. Best touring cycling routes around Villanueva De Los Infantes The most popular touring cycling route is Almedina — circular from Villanueva de los Infantes, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural surroundings of Villanueva de los Infantes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Villahermosa, a moderate 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route offers views of the local countryside and agricultural areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Main Square – Convent of Santo Domingo loop from Villanueva de los Infantes, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail leading through the historical town and its immediate vicinity, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Villanueva De Los Infantes is defined by historical towns, agricultural landscapes, and access to natural parks like Lagunas de Ruidera. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging rides. The routes in Villanueva De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than two dozen touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: There are 7 touring cycling routes available around Villanueva de los Infantes, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These include 6 moderate routes and 1 more challenging option.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while cycling around Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: Villanueva de los Infantes is rich in history. You can cycle through the town's well-preserved historical-artistic site, which features Renaissance and Baroque architecture. Specific landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Santo Domingo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Andrés&lt;/a&gt;. The town is also famously associated with Cervantes and Francisco de Quevedo.

Q: Are there any natural features or scenic viewpoints to see on a touring bike ride?
A: Yes, the nearby Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park is a significant natural draw. You can find scenic spots like the Mirador de la Laguna del Rey (King's Lagoon Viewpoint), which offers panoramic views of Laguna del Rey and Laguna de la Colgada. The access road to La Isla, between Laguna de San Pedro and Laguna del Rey, is also highlighted as a beautiful cycling path.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca – Main Square loop from Villanueva de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult. This route covers approximately 65 km with significant elevation gains, providing a good test for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Villanueva de los Infantes are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the well-paved surfaces, the blend of historical towns with agricultural landscapes, and the access to natural parks.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: Many of the routes in the area are circular, making them convenient for day trips. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057577280/almedina-circular-from-villanueva-de-los-infantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almedina — circular from Villanueva de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square – Convent of Santo Domingo loop from Villanueva de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt;. The broader region also features routes like the Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: The Campo de Montiel region, where Villanueva de los Infantes is located, generally experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling, offering milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for exploring the historical towns and natural landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, many of the moderate routes with well-paved surfaces and manageable distances could be suitable for families with some cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Villahermosa&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers views of the local countryside and agricultural areas, which can be enjoyable for a family outing.

Q: Are there options for longer distance touring cycling routes near Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: Beyond the immediate vicinity, the region offers opportunities for longer rides. The Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop, at 73.3 km, provides a substantial ride exploring historical sites. Another option is the Torrenueva from Cózar loop, which is 91.4 km, offering a longer distance for touring cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Villanueva de los Infantes?
A: The terrain around Villanueva de los Infantes primarily consists of well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bikes. While many routes traverse agricultural landscapes and charming towns, some segments may include moderate elevation gains, particularly on longer routes, requiring good fitness.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay along the cycling routes?
A: Villanueva de los Infantes itself, being a historical town, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Many routes pass through or near other charming towns in the Campo de Montiel region, where you can find places to refuel and rest during your ride.
tips: A small viewpoint with views of the fields around Almedina, belonging to the Campo de Montiel region. The axis of economy of the town is agriculture: olive trees, vines, fruit and vegetables and cereals.

The Plaza Mayor of Villanueva de los Infantes is considered one of the most beautiful main squares in Castilla-La Mancha, not in vain has it been declared a Historic-Artistic Site, with notable buildings such as the Town Hall, emblazoned mansions, porticoed gallery, the Hospital de Santiago, the Convent of Santo Domingo or the Church of San Andrés Apóstol.

waypoints.name: Plaza Mayor de Villanueva de los Infantes
